title: CMDB Mandatory Attribute Completeness by Class
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" earliest=-1d
| eval core_ok=if(isnotnull(support_group) AND support_group!="" AND isnotnull(environment) AND environment!="",1,0)
| stats avg(core_ok) as completeness_ratio, count as total by ci_class
| eval completeness_pct=round(100*completeness_ratio,1)
| sort completeness_pct
| head 30
description: Class-specific mandatory fields (owner, environment, support group) drive automation and reporting; completeness scoring by `ci_class` gives CMDB stewards actionable ITIL-aligned quality targets.
value: Class-specific mandatory fields (owner, environment, support group) drive automation and reporting; completeness scoring by `ci_class` gives CMDB stewards actionable ITIL-aligned quality targets.
implementation: (1) Expand `core_ok` with `assigned_to` or `company` if those are mandatory in your data model; (2) Exclude retired CIs with `install_status`; (3) Publish monthly scorecards and alert when any major `ci_class` drops below the governance threshold two months in a row.
visualization: Bar chart (completeness % by CI class), Table (worst classes), Line chart (completeness trend from summary index).
knownFalsePositives: Coverage gaps while onboarding new systems, after mergers, during discovery or field changes, or when teams skip owner updates in busy months.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci"` (`ci_class`, `assigned_to`, `support_group`, `environment`, `location`, `company`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Expand `core_ok` with `assigned_to` or `company` if those are mandatory in your data model; (2) Exclude retired CIs with `install_status`; (3) Publish monthly scorecards and alert when any major `ci_class` drops below the governance threshold two months in a row.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" earliest=-1d
| eval core_ok=if(isnotnull(support_group) AND support_group!="" AND isnotnull(environment) AND environment!="",1,0)
| stats avg(core_ok) as completeness_ratio, count as total by ci_class
| eval completeness_pct=round(100*completeness_ratio,1)
| sort completeness_pct
| head 30
```

Understanding this SPL

**CMDB Mandatory Attribute Completeness by Class** ‚Äî Class-specific mandatory fields (owner, environment, support group) drive automation and reporting; completeness scoring by `ci_class` gives CMDB stewards actionable ITIL-aligned quality targets.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci"` (`ci_class`, `assigned_to`, `support_group`, `environment`, `location`, `company`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:cmdb_ci. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **core_ok** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ci_class** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **completeness_pct** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow configuration item or related CMDB view. Spot-check a few CIs, owners, and relationship fields, then confirm discovery dates if we measure freshness.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (completeness % by CI class), Table (worst classes), Line chart (completeness trend from summary index).
grandmaExplanation: We check required fields for each class of thing we track, so every record is fit for the job we ask the book to do.
